dependency_notes: Stories S1–S5 are pure library code (no React, no context imports) and are independently unit-testable. S6–S8 depend on S2 (callStore) and S5 (callManager) for state management. S9 depends on S5 (callManager sends the notice). S10 depends on S1 (turnConfig module). S11 (i18n) depends on S6–S8 for its string keys to be known. S12 (e2e) depends on all other stories completing. The story_order above is a valid topological sort.
mock_resolution_plan: S1 introduces a mock for the NDK/NIP-44 encrypt and finalizeEvent surface (MOCK-01-001). S2 introduces a mock for callSignaling.subscribeCallSignaling (MOCK-02-001). S3 introduces a mock for getUserMedia (MOCK-03-001). S4 introduces a mock for RTCPeerConnection (MOCK-04-001). All four mocks are resolved by S5 (callManager integration), which wires the real modules together. UI stories S6–S8 use the real callStore and callManager from S2 and S5 respectively.

seams.data: Decoded inner call event routed from callSignaling to callManager

invariants: callId is a non-empty UUID string
kind is one of exactly six values: 25050, 25051, 25052, 25053, 25054, 25055
kind-25050 always has callType set to 'voice' or 'video'
kind-25052 always has iceCandidate set; sdpMid defaults to '0' and sdpMLineIndex to 0 if absent in wire format

seams.data: Active call state surfaced to UI components

invariants: incoming and active are never both non-null simultaneously
IncomingCall has fields: callId (string), callerPubkey (string), callType ('voice'|'video'), groupId (string|null)
ActiveCall has fields: callId (string), participants (RemoteParticipant[]), localStream (MediaStream|null), callType ('voice'|'video')
RemoteParticipant has fields: pubkey (string), stream (MediaStream|null), muted (boolean), videoOff (boolean)

seams.data: Local media stream passed from mediaManager to peerSession and CallScreen

invariants: stream contains exactly the tracks returned
releaseMedia(stream) calls stop() on every track in stream

seams.data: TURN/STUN RTCConfiguration from turnConfig to peerSession

invariants: iceServers always contains at least three STUN entries when no user config is set
iceTransportPolicy is 'relay' when IP-privacy mode is enabled, absent or 'all' otherwise
User-supplied TURN replaces the default TURN entry but STUN entries are preserved

stories.name: signaling-codec-transport
stories.title: Signaling codec and gift-wrap transport (callSignaling.ts)
stories.description: Implement callSignaling.ts: encode/decode all six AC-WebRTC inner event kinds (25050–25055), wrap/unwrap via kind-21059 gift-wrap (2-layer, no seal), freshness check (20 s), event-id dedupe (bounded set), and roster gate hook. No React imports. Reuses buildGiftWrap pattern from joinRequestSender.ts (kind 1059 → 21059) and the NDK subscribe pattern from directMessageNotifications.ts.

includes: create new file app/src/lib/calls/callSignaling.ts
create new file app/src/types/index.ts call-related types (CallEvent, IncomingCallEvent)
Encode/decode functions for all six call event kinds
kind-21059 outer wrap send path (ephemeral key per message, NIP-44 encrypt)
kind-21059 receive path: NDK subscribe, decrypt, verify, freshness, dedupe, roster gate
subscribeCallSignaling() function returning an unsubscribe handle
sendOffer/sendAnswer/sendIceCandidate/sendHangup/sendReject/sendRenegotiate helpers
excludes: RTCPeerConnection — no WebRTC in this story
callStore or callManager — no state machine
React components or hooks

introduces_mocks.description: Mock for NDK NIP-44 encrypt and finalizeEvent to allow unit testing without real NDK instance

test_fixtures.description: Pre-generated test keypairs (caller, callee1, callee2) for signing and verifying inner events in unit tests
test_fixtures.reason: Deterministic test vectors needed across all signaling tests; not an external service

stories.notes: The 2-layer wrap (signed inner event → kind-21059 outer, no seal) differs from DMs (3-layer). Adapt buildGiftWrap() from joinRequestSender.ts changing kind 1059 → 21059 and using signer.signEvent() not createRumor(). The roster gate calls getGroupMembers() which is async — the receive path must await it.

stories.name: call-store-incoming-watcher
stories.title: Call store + incoming call watcher (callStore.ts + IncomingCallWatcher.tsx)
stories.description: Implement the module-level external store (callStore.ts) for ringing/active state using useSyncExternalStore pattern, and the null-rendering IncomingCallWatcher component that mounts in Layout.tsx and drives the store from incoming signaling events. The watcher follows DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher.tsx exactly.

includes: create new file app/src/lib/calls/callStore.ts
create new file app/src/components/calls/IncomingCallWatcher.tsx
Module-level state + listeners Set + subscribe/getSnapshot following pendingInvitations.ts pattern
useCallStore() hook wrapping useSyncExternalStore
IncomingCallWatcher: null-rendering component, groupsRef pattern, dynamic import of callSignaling, cleanup
Mount IncomingCallWatcher in app/src/components/Layout.tsx alongside DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher
excludes: callManager — watcher sets store directly for S2; real callManager integration lands in S5
IncomingCallModal rendering — that is S6
Any WebRTC code

introduces_mocks.description: Stub for callSignaling.subscribeCallSignaling that fires synthetic IncomingCallEvent payloads in unit tests

stories.notes: S2 depends on S1 for the subscribeCallSignaling API shape (the seam contract). The Layout.tsx edit must add <IncomingCallWatcher /> on one line adjacent to <DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher />.

stories.name: media-manager
stories.title: Media manager (mediaManager.ts)
stories.description: Implement mediaManager.ts: acquireMedia (getUserMedia), muteAudio, disableVideo, switchCamera (replaceTrack), releaseMedia. No React, no WebRTC peer connections. Pure browser MediaStream API wrapper.

includes: create new file app/src/lib/calls/mediaManager.ts
acquireMedia(video: boolean): Promise<MediaStream>
muteAudio(stream, muted), disableVideo(stream, disabled)
switchCamera(stream, deviceId): Promise<void> using replaceTrack
releaseMedia(stream): void stopping all tracks
enumerateDevices() helper for camera/mic/output listing
Resolution/framerate constraints per participant count (§10.1)
excludes: RTCPeerConnection — no WebRTC peer connection
React components or hooks
IP-privacy mode — that is turnConfig.ts (S10)

introduces_mocks.description: Mock navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia returning a fake MediaStream with audio and video tracks for unit tests

stories.notes: Browser autoplay constraint: acquireMedia should only be called in response to a user gesture; the module itself has no enforcement mechanism — the callers (S5 callManager and S6 UI) must call it at the right moment.

stories.name: peer-session
stories.title: PeerSession: single RTCPeerConnection lifecycle (peerSession.ts)
stories.description: Implement PeerSession class encapsulating one RTCPeerConnection per remote participant. Handles createOffer, applyAnswer, addIceCandidate (with queuing), addTrack, ICE candidate emission, and ICE restart trigger. No React, no signaling transport.

includes: create new file app/src/lib/calls/peerSession.ts
PeerSession class with constructor(config: RTCConfiguration, onIceCandidate, onTrack, onConnectionStateChange)
createOffer(stream): Promise<RTCSessionDescriptionInit>
createAnswer(offerSdp): Promise<RTCSessionDescriptionInit>
applyAnswer(sdp): Promise<void>
addIceCandidate(candidate): Promise<void> with pre-answer queue
addTrack(stream): void
close(): void
ICE restart: trigger on iceConnectionState 'failed', emit a new offer via callback, max one restart per failure event
excludes: Signaling transport — no callSignaling imports
Call state machine — no callManager imports
React or UI code
adapter.js integration deferred to S5 integration (just import it in peerSession.ts)

introduces_mocks.description: Mock RTCPeerConnection (vi.fn() or manual stub) that tracks calls to createOffer, setLocalDescription, setRemoteDescription, addIceCandidate, addTrack

stories.notes: Unified Plan SDP semantics (browser default). Trickle ICE: emit each candidate via the onIceCandidate callback immediately as icecandidate event fires. ICE candidate queue must be flushed after applyAnswer or createAnswer+setRemoteDescription.

stories.name: call-manager-state-machine
stories.title: CallManager state machine (callManager.ts)
stories.description: Implement callManager.ts: the orchestrating state machine for a single call session. Coordinates callSignaling.ts (transport), PeerSession per peer (mesh), mediaManager (local stream), callStore (state publication), and enforces all call rules: mesh tiebreakers, glare resolution, ring timeout, busy-reject, group call cap, multi-device echo, and mid-call join/leave.

includes: create new file app/src/lib/calls/callManager.ts
CallManager class: startCall(groupId, targetPubkeys, callType), acceptCall(incomingCall), rejectCall(incomingCall), hangup()
State machine: idle → ringing → active → ended
Full mesh: one PeerSession per remote participant, capped at 4 (5 total including self)
Lower-pubkey-initiates rule for callee-to-callee offers
Higher-pubkey-wins rule for renegotiation glare with rollback
Ring timeout: 45 seconds, implemented with setTimeout
Busy-reject: auto-reject new offers when already active
Multi-device echo: Answer and Reject also wrapped to self pubkey
Mid-call join: existing peers unconditionally initiate offers to newcomer
Group call notice publish at call-start and call-end via applicationRumorDispatcher
Cleanup: releaseMedia and close all PeerSessions on end
excludes: React or UI components
ICE config reads — callManager calls turnConfig.getConfig() but does not own turnConfig.ts (S10)
i18n strings — no user-visible strings in callManager.ts

stories.notes: This story resolves all four temporary mocks by wiring the real modules together. Use vi.fn() spies on callSignaling send functions to assert correct events are dispatched. The applicationRumorDispatcher integration for call notices (AC-NOTICE-1, AC-NOTICE-2) requires importing the dispatcher from app/src/lib/marmot/applicationRumorDispatcher.ts.

stories.name: incoming-call-modal
stories.title: Incoming call modal UI (IncomingCallModal.tsx)
stories.description: Implement IncomingCallModal.tsx: a Chakra UI Modal that renders when callStore has a non-null incoming call. Shows caller identity (display name resolved from Nostr profile), call type (Voice/Video), Accept and Decline buttons. Accept triggers callManager.acceptCall; Decline triggers callManager.rejectCall.

includes: create new file app/src/components/calls/IncomingCallModal.tsx
useCallStore() to read incoming call state
Nostr profile resolution for caller display name
Chakra UI Modal (isOpen tied to incoming !== null)
Accept: call callManager.acceptCall(incoming), request media permissions
Decline: call callManager.rejectCall(incoming)
Ring timeout visual indicator (countdown from 45 s)
i18n strings for all displayed text (en + de, delegated to S11 for string definitions)
excludes: Active call UI — that is S7 (CallScreen)
Permission-denial error handling — surface is in CallScreen/callManager, not the modal

stories.notes: Depends on S2 (callStore) and S5 (callManager.acceptCall/rejectCall). The modal should not call getUserMedia directly; that is callManager.acceptCall's responsibility.

stories.name: active-call-screen
stories.title: Active call screen UI (CallScreen.tsx)
stories.description: Implement CallScreen.tsx: the in-call view showing per-participant tiles (video stream or avatar), mute/camera/hang-up controls, and per-peer connection quality indicators. Renders when callStore.active is non-null.

includes: create new file app/src/components/calls/CallScreen.tsx
useCallStore() for active call state
Per-participant tile: <video> element for video calls, avatar for voice
Speaking indicator per tile (audio level detection via AudioContext, best-effort)
Controls: mute toggle, camera on/off (video calls only), hang-up button
Per-peer connection quality indicator (RTCPeerConnection.getStats, simplified: good/degraded/failed)
Participant-count overlay (N/5)
Picture-in-Picture hook (requestPictureInPicture on main video element, best-effort)
excludes: Incoming call modal — that is S6
Start call entry point — that is S8
i18n string definitions — delegated to S11

stories.notes: Depends on S2 (callStore), S3 (mediaManager mute/camera helpers), and S5 (callManager.hangup). Remote streams arrive via callStore.active.participants[n].stream — callManager sets these when PeerSession fires onTrack.

stories.name: start-call-entry-point
stories.title: Start call entry points in group and contact views
stories.description: Add Voice and Video call initiation buttons to the existing group view (app/pages/groups.tsx) and the 1:1 contact/DM view. Each button calls callManager.startCall() with the appropriate target pubkeys and call type.

includes: Add Voice and Video call buttons to app/pages/groups.tsx group detail view
Add Voice and Video call buttons to the 1:1 contact/DM view
Optional participant picker for group calls (subset of roster, ≤4 others)
'Call full' indicator when participant count is 5
Disable call buttons when already in an active call
Wire buttons to callManager.startCall(groupId, targetPubkeys, callType)
excludes: CallScreen rendering — already in S7; groups.tsx just needs to show CallScreen when active
Permission handling — that is callManager and mediaManager

stories.notes: S8 also serves as the integration point that unifies S5–S7: the full outgoing call flow (start → ringing → active → ended) is first exercised end-to-end here. The e2e tests in S12 depend on S8 being complete. owning_module is CallScreen because the start-call entry is part of the call UI surface; groups.tsx owns its own group-management concerns but the call initiation concern is owned here.

stories.name: group-call-notice
stories.title: Group call notice handler (kind-445 rumor)
stories.description: Implement the call_notice rumor type in the applicationRumorDispatcher pipeline: encode/decode kind-445 application rumors with type 'call_notice', and wire callManager to publish start/end notices.

includes: create new file app/src/lib/calls/callNoticeHandler.ts
callNoticeHandler: handles incoming call_notice rumors from other members (display in group timeline)
Encode call_notice rumor payload (started/ended) and publish via applicationRumorDispatcher
Register callNoticeHandler in app/src/lib/marmot/registerHandlers.ts
CALL_NOTICE_RUMOR_KIND constant
excludes: UI rendering of call notices in the group timeline — out of scope for this story; just publish the rumor
Ringing or signaling — this is group-timeline state only

stories.notes: Depends on S5 (callManager publishes via this module). Follows the existing handler registration pattern in registerHandlers.ts. The handler needs to be registered once at app startup.

stories.name: call-settings-panel
stories.title: TURN config and IP-privacy settings panel (settings.tsx + turnConfig.ts)
stories.description: Implement turnConfig.ts (read/write lp_turnConfig_v1) and add the 'Call Settings' section to app/pages/settings.tsx with TURN URL, username, credential inputs, and IP-privacy toggle.

includes: create new file app/src/lib/calls/turnConfig.ts
getConfig(): RTCConfiguration — reads lp_turnConfig_v1, returns defaults + user overrides
saveConfig(config): void — writes to lp_turnConfig_v1
Default shipped STUN/TURN config constants
IP-privacy mode flag (stored in lp_turnConfig_v1)
Add 'Call Settings' section to app/pages/settings.tsx
i18n strings for settings labels (en + de, delegated to S11)
excludes: peerSession.ts ICE restart — that is S4
Any call UI other than settings

stories.notes: The default TURN server URL and credential must be defined as constants in turnConfig.ts — the actual TURN server to ship is a deployment decision. Use a placeholder constant (e.g. TURN_DEFAULT_URL = '') that the operator fills in before release. S4 (peerSession) consumes the IceConfigResult seam defined here.

stories.name: i18n-call-strings
stories.title: i18n completeness: all call-related strings in en + de
stories.description: Add all call-related i18n keys to app/src/lib/i18n.ts (both en and de locales). Keys cover: incoming call modal, active call controls, call settings panel, permission-denied errors, call ended/missed state labels, and group call notice text.

includes: Add call i18n keys to the Copy type in app/src/lib/i18n.ts
Populate en and de values for every new key
Unit test asserting all call keys are present in both locales and values differ between en and de
excludes: Translations for non-call features
Any component or logic changes

stories.notes: owning_module is set to callSignaling (the signaling lib module) because there is no separate i18n module in the architecture; the call strings logically belong to the calls/ sub-system. S11 should be done after S6–S10 so all string keys needed by the UI are known. The Copy type is strict TypeScript — missing either locale causes a compile error, so the unit test is the compile itself plus a runtime check that en !== de per key.

stories.name: e2e-ring-decline
stories.title: E2E: outgoing call rings and decline flow
stories.description: Playwright e2e test covering: (1) a 1:1 voice call that connects (both peers reach active state) and tears down on hangup; (2) a decline flow where callee declines and caller sees the ended state. Uses two browser contexts on the same machine with loopback ICE (no TURN required in CI).

includes: create new file app/tests/e2e/calls/ring-and-decline.test.ts
Two-context Playwright test: caller context and callee context
Loopback ICE configuration (no TURN required)
Test bridge window.__nostlingAnswerCall and window.__nostlingRejectCall for callee interaction
Assert CallScreen appears for caller after answer; assert call ends after hangup
Assert incoming call modal appears for callee; Decline triggers state change on caller
excludes: Group call e2e (≥3 parties) — manual only for now
TURN/NAT resilience tests — manual only (AC-RES-1, AC-RES-2)
Amethyst interop test — manual only (AC-INTEROP-1)

test_fixtures.description: Loopback ICE-only RTCConfiguration (no STUN, no TURN) for CI: forces all ICE candidates to loopback addresses, ensuring the test works without external servers
test_fixtures.interface: RTCConfiguration with iceServers: [] and iceTransportPolicy omitted
test_fixtures.reason: External STUN/TURN servers are unavailable in CI; loopback candidates work on the same machine

stories.notes: Depends on all S1–S11 stories completing. The e2e test must publish through the app (via callManager.startCall invoked through a test bridge), not via raw WebSocket to strfry. The loopback ICE fixture overrides the TURN config for the test contexts only. AC-RES-1, AC-RES-2, and AC-INTEROP-1 are manual-only and are not blocked on this story.
